title: elaphoglossum eggersii
intro: Elaphoglossum eggersii, also known as Eggers' Elaphoglossum, is a species of epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum eggersii include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is then consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
